elements.sent-2411-005: Key words: Blow-up rate; Quantitative analysis; Carleman inequalities; Critical space; Pigeonhole principle.
elements.sent-2411-006: The purpose of this article is to study the explicit quantification of the blow-up rate with respect to time of the critical norm near a potential singularity for the Magneto-Hydrodynamics (MHD for short) equations, which is a branch of continuum mechanics that examines the flow of electrically conducting fluids under the influence of magnetic fields.
elements.sent-2411-007: Due to its numerous practical applications, such as magnetic separation and targeted delivery of drugs or radioisotopes via magnetic guidance, research on magnetohydrodynamics has attracted widespread attention.
elements.sent-2411-008: The movement of conductive fluids (such as liquid metal mercury, liquid sodium, and plasma) in a magnetic field is a result of the coupling of these two fundamental forces.
elements.sent-2411-009: Firstly, fluid motion generates an electric current that modifies the existing magnetic field.
elements.sent-2411-010: Secondly, the interaction between the current and the magnetic field produces a mechanical force in the fluid, accelerating it in a direction perpendicular to both the magnetic field and the current.
elements.sent-2411-011: Therefore, in mathematical physics, the MHD equations are derived by coupling the Navier-Stokes equations from fluid mechanics with Maxwell's equations from electromagnetic fields.
elements.sent-2411-012: For more physical background on the MHD equation, we refer the interested readers to the reference \cite{MR1393572, MR98556, MR346289 .}

elements.sent-2411-018: In \cite{MR1555394 , Leray used the principle of extremal values for integral equations to prove that if the existence interval of a smooth solution $v$ is a finite interval $(0,T)$, where the first blow-up time of the solution is $t=T$, then it must exhibit the following blow-up behavior: \begin{equation}\label{ns.quan1} \|v(t)\|_{L_x^p(\R^3)}\geq\frac{c(p)}{(T-t)^{\frac12(1-\frac3p)}},\quad p\in(3,\infty],\end{equation} with some $c(p)$ depending only on $p$.}
elements.sent-2411-019: However, it, for the critical case $p=3$, is extremely complicated.
elements.sent-2411-020: In fact, the qualitative analysis about the $L^3(\R^3)$ norm of potential singularity solutions at the maximum existence time $T$ has been a well-known open problem for a long time, where the time $T$ is finite, i.e. whether \begin{equation}\label{ns.case1} \limsup\limits_{t\rightarrow T}\|v(t)\|_{L_x^3(\R^3)}=\infty\end{equation} holds true or equivalently whether \begin{equation}\label{ns.case2} v\in L^\infty(0,T; L^3(\R^3)) \Rightarrow v~is~regular~at~t=T.\end{equation}.
